item.title: 13th Year of Blogging
item.description: I've been blogging for 13 years. So much has changed, yet everything is the same. The only thing AI cannot replicate is longevity. Plant your seeds right now.

item.title: Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor
item.description: Thoughts on an experiment that I've been running for about a month.

item.title: Software Engineering Is Becoming Civil Engineering
item.description: I gave a guest lecture on AI in Michael Hilton’s Foundations of Software Engineering course (CMU 17-313) today. One of my favorite things about lecturing is the conversations that happen afterward, the ones that go in directions nobody planned.

item.title: Optimizing for understanding
item.description: When code review is the biggest bottleneck, you need to optimize for understanding.

item.title: Translating non-trivial codebases with Claude
item.description: In my last post, I stated:

item.title: Signals, the push-pull based algorithm — Willy Brauner
item.description: We have been using Signals in production for years via several modern front-end frameworks like Solid, Vue, and others, but few of us are able to explain how they work internally. I wanted to dig into it, especially diving deep into the push-pull based algorithm, the core mechanism behind their reactivity.

item.title: Kasava - The Agentic Platform for Product Engineers
item.description: Kasava is the AI-native platform purpose-built for product development. Plan, build, and ship with AI-powered chat, call analysis, and plan generation.

item.title: Why You (Probably) Don't Need Static Typing in Rails
item.description: Static typing in Ruby sounds appealing — but good naming conventions get you 80% of the benefits with none of the overhead. No gems, no annotations, no build step.
item.author: (Philippe Durix)

item.title: Paste clipboard images into Claude Code over SSH
item.description: Claude Code can read images, but not when you're connected over SSH. claude-ssh-image-skill bridges that gap with a local daemon, a Go client, and an SSH reverse tunnel.

item.title: Reading leaked Claude Code source code
item.description: Interesting findings from reading 132,000 lines of TypeScript that power Claude Code. The post uses the repo at https://github.com/chatgptprojects/claude-code to reference code, note that it might go down by the time you are reading this.

item.title: The Struggle is What We Crave
item.description: On the death of web dev communities, the callouses that made us better, and why eliminating struggle might be the worst thing we've ever built.

item.title: Speed Is Only Useful If You're Going in the Right Direction — Matthew Boston
item.description: Velocity is a vector — it has magnitude and direction. AI tools make it trivially easy to build the wrong thing fast. The bottleneck was never typing speed; it was always understanding.
item.author: (Matthew Boston)

item.title: Why Are There So Many Dead Posts on Hacker News | Kruno Golubić
item.description: Some platforms are silently banned on Hacker News. Your posts go dead and you never know. If this matters to you, here's what you can do about it.

item.title: Birthday Slideshow | Joe IQ
item.description: Throwing a birthday party for yourself is the perfect way to get your favorite people in one room. And while guests have a great time during the party, the joy doesn’t stop when it’s over: they’re still raving about your slideshow days later. What slideshow? I can explain…

item.title: Agent Frameworks Are Getting Squeezed
item.description: AI labs are building down into orchestration. Automation platforms are building up into reasoning. Agent frameworks are caught in a compression event from both sides.

item.title: I Am a Cross-Cutting Concern — Scott Lawson
item.description: Everything I've built in the last decade lives in one repo. Not because the projects are related, but because I am.

item.title: Very small engines
item.description: One of the interesting things to contemplate is the scale of the internal combustion engine. It’s a very human scale device; pistons the size of fists, Valves about as wide as knuckles. It&#8…

item.title: DRAM pricing is killing the hobbyist SBC market
item.description: Today Raspberry Pi announced more price increases for all Pis with LPDDR4 RAM, alongside a 'right-sized' 3GB RAM Pi 4 for $83.75. The price increases bring the 16GB Pi 5 up to $299.99. Despite today's date, this is not a joke. I published a video going over the state of the hobbyist 'high end SBC' market (4/8/16 GB models in the current generation), which I'll embed below: But if you'd like the tl;dr:

item.title: Creation
NASA’s Artemis II Crew Launches to the Moon (Official Broadcast)
item.description: Artemis II is NASA’s first crewed mission under the Artemis program and will launch from the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida. It will send NASA

item.title: Introducing EmDash — the spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security
item.description: Today we are launching the beta of EmDash, a full-stack serverless JavaScript CMS built on Astro 6.0. It combines the features of a traditional CMS with modern security, running plugins in sandboxed Worker isolates.

item.title: I quit. The clankers won.
item.description: The one where I pack up my bags

item.title: What is Copilot exactly?
item.description: A coworker of mine told me that he uses Microsoft Copilot frequently. In fact, he said "I don't know how I did my work without it." That came as a surprise to me. I can't stand Copilot. This is a very

item.title: Why It Sucks To Be Born as a Software Engineer
item.description:
Happy April.
Don't forget to comment, like and subscribe :)
Social Media: Follow me on GitHub: https://github.com/Elfocrash Follow me on Twitter: https://twitter.com/nickchapsas Connect on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nick-chapsas
item.author: (Nick Chapsas)

item.title: PrismML — Concentrating intelligence
item.description: Large models can't fit on smartphones. Datacenters can't sustain them. PrismML is building ultra dense intelligence to solve both.

item.title: Claude Code Unpacked
item.description: What actually happens when you type a message into Claude Code? The agent loop, 40+ tools, multi-agent orchestration, and unreleased features, mapped from source.

item.title: Joins are NOT Expensive! - Raw Reading
item.description: Joins are NOT Expensive! - Raw ReadingWhen talking about Data Lakes and how people access them - we must address some of the misconceptions that made them popular in the first place.
